scope: ProtocolLab QUIC, HTTP/3, WebSocket-over-HTTP/3, and benchmark-package candidate inventory.

sourceModel.ecosystemBaseline: QUIC interop runner peers with Docker endpoint artifacts are treated as runnable candidates.
sourceModel.protocolLabBoundary: ProtocolLab component package metadata belongs in protocol-lab-components; executable lab targets and local adapter wiring remain in protocol-lab-internal or implementation-owned repositories.
sourceModel.siteBoundary: The public site catalog mirrors visibility only. It must not imply production certification or benchmark acceptance.

statusVocabulary.packaged: A ProtocolLab package or implementation-owned package surface already exists.
statusVocabulary.internal-target: A protocol-lab-internal target/adapter exists but is not a protocol-lab-components package.
statusVocabulary.interop-image: A QUIC interop runner image exists and can seed a package wrapper.
statusVocabulary.external: Upstream project is viable, but ProtocolLab has no runnable component package yet.
statusVocabulary.planned: Useful candidate, but the next package slice needs design or build work before runnable packaging.
statusVocabulary.blocked: Known licensing, protocol, role, or build-practicality issue blocks an immediate package.

topNextPackageSlices: Broader raw QUIC ecosystem targets after quic-go raw stream-throughput, multiplex, and duplex package proof remains stable.
Caddy and nginx large-body/header-heavy fixture expansion after current package proofs remain stable.
quic-go HTTP/3 client or QPACK-focused extension package after the server baseline remains stable.
xquic interop-image package for QUIC plus HTTP/3 client/server coverage.

implementations.license: Incursa-owned; package feasibility depends on repository release policy.
implementations.dockerImageAvailability: Implementation-owned/local ProtocolLab artifacts; protocol-lab-internal has process adapters for Incursa raw QUIC and HTTP/3 plus a local Docker target for Incursa HTTP/3.
implementations.quicRawSupport: yes; Incursa raw QUIC adapter covers handshake, echo, bulk, multiplexing, and duplex scenarios.
implementations.http3Support: yes; Incursa HTTP/3 target covers public HTTP application scenarios.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: high for canonical local ProtocolLab proof; publishability remains evidence-class dependent.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: implementation-owned package surface; internal adapters exist in protocol-lab-internal.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Keep implementation-owned packages aligned with raw QUIC scenario pack and executor packages from protocol-lab-components.

implementations.dockerImageAvailability: QUIC interop runner lists ghcr.io/microsoft/msquic/qns:main. ProtocolLab also has an immutable, self-contained Linux x64 package for the implementation-owned process-backed .NET raw QUIC adapter.
implementations.quicRawSupport: yes; general-purpose QUIC library and ProtocolLab raw adapter path.
implementations.http3Support: not directly in MSQuic; Kestrel and other stacks layer HTTP/3 over MsQuic.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: high for transport-level baselines and adapter comparisons.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: implementation-owned raw adapter package live-proven with an explicit libmsquic capability gate and clean-source build provenance.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Add isolated-role and decision-ready common-cohort proof; keep the implementation package owned by protocol-lab-internal and the shared executor/scenarios owned by protocol-lab-components.

implementations.dockerImageAvailability: QUIC interop runner lists martenseemann/quic-go-interop:latest. ProtocolLab packages a quic-go raw QUIC target, a quic-go raw load executor, and a quic-go HTTP/3 server implementation.
implementations.http3Support: yes; quic-go documents HTTP/3, QPACK, HTTP Datagrams, and related extensions.
implementations.webSocketOverH3Support: not in core package; related webtransport-go covers WebTransport over HTTP/3.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: high for raw QUIC target and load generation plus HTTP/3 server baseline smoke; publishable leaderboard claims still need isolated/repeated evidence.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: raw QUIC target packaged as org.protocol-lab.components.implementation.quic-go-raw for cold handshake, connection churn, stream churn, throughput, multiplex, and duplex scenarios; raw QUIC executor packaged; HTTP/3 server implementation packaged as org.protocol-lab.components.implementation.quic-go-http3 with live H3 1KB validation proof.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Extend quic-go raw only after additional raw scenarios have live package-backed proof; keep HTTP/3 expansion in the quic-go-http3 package.

implementations.dockerImageAvailability: QUIC interop runner lists aiortc/aioquic-qns:latest. ProtocolLab has aioquic HTTP/3 and RFC9220 WebSocket-over-H3 packages.
implementations.webSocketOverH3Support: yes via ProtocolLab aioquic RFC9220 executor.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good for compatibility and feature conformance; lower priority for throughput baselines.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: packaged implementation and packaged WebSocket-over-H3 executor.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Extend the HTTP/3 wrapper from protocol/QPACK coverage into payload and upload scenarios if needed.

implementations.dockerImageAvailability: QUIC interop runner lists cloudflare/quiche-qns:latest. ProtocolLab has a quiche HTTP/3 wrapper package.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good for HTTP/3 external comparison once wrapper routes are stable.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Add protocol/QPACK and WebSocket-over-H3 feature probes only after the current payload wrapper is stable.

implementations.dockerImageAvailability: QUIC interop runner lists ghcr.io/ngtcp2/ngtcp2-interop:latest and a WebTransport branch image. ProtocolLab has an ngtcp2 HTTP/3 wrapper package.
implementations.quicRawSupport: yes; ngtcp2 transport examples include interop-specific HQ endpoints.
implementations.http3Support: yes through nghttp3; nghttp3 also implements QPACK.
implementations.webSocketOverH3Support: supported by nghttp3 library, but not packaged in ProtocolLab.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: high as a standards-oriented C baseline; build complexity is higher than Go/Python wrappers.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Add RFC9220/WebSocket-over-H3 test coverage after HTTP/3 payload package proof remains stable.

implementations.dockerImageAvailability: ProtocolLab internal has a local Docker HTTP/3 target; upstream Caddy has official container distribution outside this inventory.
implementations.quicRawSupport: no; Caddy is an HTTP server built on quic-go.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: high for recognizable HTTP/3 server baseline; not raw QUIC.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: HTTP/1 package exists in components; HTTP/3 is an internal target and public site stub.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Promote the internal Caddy HTTP/3 Docker target into a protocol-lab-components package.

implementations.license: MIT through ASP.NET Core/.NET runtime components.
implementations.dockerImageAvailability: ProtocolLab has both component wrapper and internal target paths; HTTP/3 depends on MsQuic platform requirements.
implementations.quicRawSupport: no direct raw QUIC target; uses MsQuic under ASP.NET Core HTTP/3.
implementations.http3Support: yes; HTTP/3 is fully supported in .NET 7 and later and depends on MsQuic.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: packaged HTTP/3 implementation wrapper and internal benchmark target.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Keep package and internal target metadata aligned around platform gates and supported scenario IDs.

implementations.license: nginx open source license; packaging should use external image/source terms review.
implementations.dockerImageAvailability: ProtocolLab packages a repo-local image based on nginx:1.29.0-alpine with nginx -V HTTP/3 module proof; protocol-lab-internal also has a local nginx HTTP/3 Docker target.
implementations.http3Support: yes; nginx documents QUIC/HTTP/3 support since 1.25.0 and Linux binary package inclusion.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: high for external server baseline once module proof and TLS policy are captured.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: packaged HTTP/1 and HTTP/3 implementation wrappers; HTTP/3 package preserves nginx -V module proof in internal metadata.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Register the nginx-http3 package with the live controller and record package-backed H3 smoke evidence when worker Docker capability allows it.

implementations.quicRawSupport: Upstream transport support exists, but the reviewed image has no exact ProtocolLab raw-stream mapping; the retained raw feasibility decision records its Initial-level frame failure under the canonical adapter.
implementations.webSocketOverH3Support: not identified for ProtocolLab package scope.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good external interop peer; package should begin as compatibility/interop before throughput claims.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: server-only HTTP/3 peer-characterization package org.protocol-lab.components.implementation.xquic-http3 and paired XQUIC executor pass package-local status smoke; canonical payload is not claimed and real-controller proof remains pending.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Run the immutable package and paired executor through the real controller, retain the response-FIN warning, and publish diagnostic status evidence.

implementations.http3Support: not a primary ProtocolLab HTTP/3 target in this inventory.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good compact transport interop peer; lower immediate HTTP/3 value.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Add raw QUIC interop package for handshake, transfer, and stream behavior before HTTP/3.

implementations.license: MIT plus repository notices; review bundled LICENSE.chrome before redistributing binaries.
implementations.quicRawSupport: Upstream transport support exists, but the reviewed image exposes HQ semantics rather than the canonical ProtocolLab raw echo contract; the retained raw feasibility decision records the zero-byte response.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good external QUIC/H3 implementation; binary redistribution review needed.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: server-only HTTP/3 peer-characterization package org.protocol-lab.components.implementation.lsquic-http3 passes package-local status smoke; canonical payload is not claimed and real-controller proof remains pending.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Run the immutable package through the real controller with the corrected curl HTTP/3 executor and publish diagnostic status evidence.

implementations.quicRawSupport: yes; mvfst is a QUIC transport implementation.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: strong transport peer, but build/dependency complexity makes it design-first.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: not packaged as an HTTP/3 origin: the pinned Proxygen HQ static sample serves H3 but omits Content-Type on the required status and byte paths.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Re-open only for an immutable upstream typed-response surface or an explicitly approved, separately maintained native Proxygen application adapter.

implementations.dockerImageAvailability: No active QUIC interop runner image in the reviewed implementation list.
implementations.http3Support: yes, but upstream labels HTTP/3 experimental.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: interesting server peer, but not a first benchmark baseline without a maintained image.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Find or build a maintained container with explicit HTTP/3 enablement before adding benchmark scenarios.

implementations.license: GPL-2.0-or-later for HAProxy, LGPL-2.1 for headers; ProtocolLab consumes the unmodified official digest-pinned image and adds repository-owned configuration and fixtures.
implementations.dockerImageAvailability: Official haproxy:3.2.21 image is pinned by digest and reports USE_QUIC=1.
implementations.http3Support: packaged as a QUIC-enabled HTTP/3 reverse-proxy listener with a separate loopback fixture backend.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: gateway cohort only; must remain separate from origin-server comparisons.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: Packaged as org.protocol-lab.components.implementation.haproxy-http-gateway with separate h1, h2, and h3 proxy identities.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Run the exact package through the real controller and publish gateway-cohort evidence.

implementations.license: Mozilla project licensing; verify exact repo license before redistribution.
implementations.quicRawSupport: Upstream transport support exists, but the reviewed image exposes HTTP/3 semantics rather than the canonical ProtocolLab raw echo contract; the retained raw feasibility decision records remote stream cancellation.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good browser-adjacent interop peer; package after core requested implementations.
implementations.protocolLabComponentStatus: server-only HTTP/3 peer-characterization package org.protocol-lab.components.implementation.neqo-http3 passes package-local status smoke; canonical payload is not claimed and real-controller proof remains pending.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Run the immutable package through the real controller with the corrected curl HTTP/3 executor and publish diagnostic status evidence.

implementations.license: Rust project license review required before redistribution.
implementations.http3Support: not primary in ProtocolLab package scope; generally used as transport library.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good raw QUIC transport peer after MSQuic/quic-go/picoquic coverage.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Raw QUIC transport wrapper seeded from the interop image.

implementations.license: Apache-2.0 expected for AWS s2n projects; verify exact repo license before redistribution.
implementations.http3Support: not primary in ProtocolLab package scope.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: strong raw QUIC candidate; useful after current transport package lane is stable.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Raw QUIC transport wrapper with source/license verification and scenario support map.

implementations.license: MIT-family quic-go ecosystem; verify exact repo license before redistribution.
implementations.http3Support: yes as WebTransport over HTTP/3 substrate.
implementations.webSocketOverH3Support: no; WebTransport is adjacent, not RFC9220 WebSocket-over-H3.
implementations.benchmarkSuitability: good future WebTransport scenario peer, not a core HTTP/3 payload baseline.
implementations.nextPackageSlice: Only package after ProtocolLab schedules WebTransport scenarios and separates them from RFC9220 WebSocket-over-H3.
